steps.title: Read the graphed line
steps.content: The graph of $y=f(x)+13$ passes through $(-5,8)$ and $(0,3)$. The slope is $$\frac{3-8}{0-(-5)}=\frac{-5}{5}=-1,$$ and the $y$-intercept is $3$, so the shown line is $y=-x+3$.

steps.title: Solve for f(x)
steps.content: The graph is $y=f(x)+13$, so subtract 13 from the shown equation: $$f(x)+13=-x+3$$ $$f(x)=-x+3-13=-x-10$$

steps.title: Match the form with positive c, d
Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $-d-cx$ has a negative intercept and negative slope, matching $f(x)=-x-10$.
  • B) $d-cx$ has the right slope sign but a positive intercept.
  • C) $d+cx$ has a positive intercept and positive slope.
  • D) $-d+cx$ has the right intercept sign but a positive slope.
That matches choice A, so A is correct.
